name: River current over boulders
note: FIRST ABIOTIC SOUNDSCAPE IN ENGINE. No species. No taxonomy. Physical process: water + gravity + stone.

physics.primary_mechanism: Turbulent flow over irregular boulder substrate
physics.polyrhythm_definition: Each boulder creates independent acoustic event at distinct frequency/amplitude. Superposition = genuine polyrhythm in mathematical sense.
physics.frequency_profile: Broadband: 20 Hz to 20 kHz+. Energy concentrated 200-2000 Hz.

pnw_rivers.name: Skykomish River
pnw_rivers.character: Steep gradient, glacial-outwash boulders, high snowmelt volume

pnw_rivers.name: Snoqualmie River (above Falls)
pnw_rivers.character: Rapids and boulder fields descending to falls (degree 246)

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST ABIOTIC SOUNDSCAPE: the engine's most significant ontological boundary. Degrees 0-244 = living organisms (sounds have biological function). Degrees 245+ = physical processes (sounds have no function, only physics). Family is now Hydrology, not taxonomy.
theory_nodes.description: The abiotic transition: from biology to physics.

theory_nodes.concept: Turbulence acoustics: eddy-scale determines frequency. Small eddies = high frequency; large eddies = low frequency. Multiple boulders = multiple simultaneous frequencies = genuine polyrhythm. Physics produces music without biological intermediary.
theory_nodes.description: How turbulent flow generates polyrhythmic sound.

theory_nodes.concept: Acoustic spine of the watershed: the river's broadband sound is the constant against which all riparian biological sounds are layered. Streamside species (American Dipper, SPS 52) evolved calls pitched above the masking frequency of stream noise. The river shaped the evolution of its neighbors' voices.
theory_nodes.description: The river as evolutionary pressure on riparian bird vocalization.

cross_references.relationship: The same river system, different mode: turbulent current (245) vs. 268-foot plunge (246). Current = polyrhythm; falls = white-noise wall.
The dipper sings next to and in fast-moving water. Its song evolved to penetrate the masking noise of mountain streams. Cross-reference: biotic response to abiotic acoustic environment.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=5 + E=8. FIRST ABIOTIC meets ENGINE MAXIMUM. Polyrhythmic water over stone pairs with polyrhythmic guitar over rhythm section. Both generate complex, multilayered sound from physical interaction. Both irreducible.

milestones: Ontological category boundary: biology to physics

human_response.psychoacoustics: Water sound reduces cortisol, lowers heart rate, induces relaxation
